2604.24376.title: Learning Evidence of Depression Symptoms via Prompt Induction
2604.24376.paper_summary: Depression places substantial pressure on mental health services, and many people describe their experiences outside clinical settings in high-volume user-generated text (e.g., online forums and social media). Automatically identifying clinical symptom evidence in such text can therefore complement limited clinical capacity and scale to large populations. We address this need through sentence-level classification of 21 depression symptoms from the BDI-II questionnaire, using BDI-Sen, a dataset annotated for symptom relevance. This task is fine-grained and highly imbalanced, and we find that common LLM approaches (zero-shot, in-context learning, and fine-tuning) struggle to apply consistent relevance criteria for most symptoms. We propose Symptom Induction (SI), a novel approach which compresses labeled examples into short, interpretable guidelines that specify what counts as evidence for each symptom and uses these guidelines to condition classification. Across four LLM families and eight models, SI achieves the best overall weighted F1 on BDI-Sen, with especially large gains for infrequent symptoms. Cross-domain evaluation on an external dataset further shows that induced guidelines generalize across other diseases shared symptomatology (bipolar and eating disorders).
2604.24376.paper_summary_zh: 抑鬱症對心理健康服務造成了重大壓力，許多人在臨床環境之外描述他們的經歷，這些經歷以高量的用戶生成文本（例如，線上論壇和社交媒體）呈現。因此，自動識別這些文本中的臨床症狀證據可以補充有限的臨床能力並擴展到大規模人群。我們通過對BDI-II問卷中21種抑鬱症狀進行句子級別的分類來解決這一需求，使用BDI-Sen，一個針對症狀相關性進行註釋的數據集。這項任務是細緻且高度不平衡的，我們發現常見的LLM方法（零樣本、上下文學習和微調）在對大多數症狀應用一致的相關性標準方面存在困難。我們提出了症狀誘導（SI），這是一種新穎的方法，將標記的例子壓縮成簡短的可解釋指導方針，這些方針具體說明了什麼算作每個症狀的證據，並利用這些指導方針來調整分類。在四個LLM家族和八個模型中，SI在BDI-Sen上實現了最佳的加權F1分數，對於不常見的症狀尤其有顯著增益。在外部數據集上的跨領域評估進一步顯示，誘導的指導方針在其他共享症狀學的疾病（如雙相情感障礙和飲食失調）中具有泛化能力。
2604.24376.author: Eliseo Bao et.al.
2604.24376.authors: Eliseo Bao, Anxo Perez, David Otero, Javier Parapar
